---
title: "Study Title"
description: "One sentence on the question and the method. This feeds the card on the work page and the meta description, so make it concrete and specific, not aspirational."
date: 2026-01-01
categories: [research, IBD, Bayesian, MRE, UC]
image: "thumb.png"
draft: true
---

::: {.project-card}

*The one-line question the study answers, in italics. Phrase it as the clinical or methodological problem, not the result.*

---

## Background

The gap this study addresses. State the clinical context and the standard of care, then name what is missing: the evidence, the resolution, or the modelling framework the field currently lacks. Two to three short paragraphs. Lead with substance; an endorser should grasp the niche and the stakes here.

---

## The Model

The methodological core. Name the outcome variable and why it is modelled the way it is. State the design choices as decisions with reasons: why ordinal rather than binary, why multilevel, why Bayesian. Specificity is the signal; name the actual structure, predictors, and the level each enters at.

---

## What It Shows

The results, stated plainly. Lead with the effect sizes or estimates that carry the argument, with intervals. Distinguish what is a headline finding from what is a robustness check. Where a number is contested or hard-won, say so; calibrated honesty reads as rigour.

---

## Why It Matters

What the study offers the field that it did not have before. Tie it to the clinical workflow or the guideline it speaks to. State the contribution without overclaiming: every sentence here must be supported by the result above.

---

## Forward

Where this is in its lifecycle: manuscript in preparation, under review, presented. Name the venue and the target if known. *[CONFIRM: preprint or submission target — add date if known.]*

---

*Presentation or publication line. Venue, location, date.*

:::
